SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.dashboard.config

Class DashboardAutoConfiguration

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.dashboard.config.DashboardAutoConfiguration
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer
@AutoConfiguration(afterName={"org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.MetricsAutoConfiguration","org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.CompositeMeterRegistryAutoConfiguration","org.springframework.boot.micrometer.metrics.autoconfigure.export.simple.SimpleMetricsExportAutoConfiguration","com.societegenerale.failover.configuration.FailoverAutoConfiguration"}) @ConditionalOnWebApplication(type=SERVLET) @ConditionalOnClass(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry.class) @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="failover.dashboard", name="enabled", havingValue="true") @EnableConfigurationProperties(DashboardProperties.class) public class DashboardAutoConfiguration extends Object implements org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer
Autoconfiguration for the embedded failover dashboard.

Secure-by-default (design doc §1a): an absent failover.dashboard.enabled property means off — no matchIfMissing. The consumer must explicitly opt in via YAML. With the property unset, no bean, controller, resource handler, JSON, or UI is mapped at all.

All dashboard surfaces share the single DashboardProperties.basePath() namespace: the static UI is served from basePath/** and the JSON API is mapped under basePath/api. The validated base path is used verbatim by both, so they cannot collide with consumer services or drift apart.

P0/P1 scope: master switch, properties, static-asset resource handler, and the config API/view. Metrics services and UI arrive in P2–P3.

Author:
Anand Manissery

    Description

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addInterceptors(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.InterceptorRegistry registry)
    Enforces exposure.include narrowing and the CSP header on every base-path/** request.
    void
    addResourceHandlers(@NonNull org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ResourceHandlerRegistry registry)
     
    void
    addViewControllers(@NonNull org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ViewControllerRegistry registry)
    Welcome mapping: a bare base-path (and its trailing-slash form) forwards to the static index.html, so /failover-dashboard opens the UI without the explicit file name.
    ClusterSeriesSampler
    clusterSeriesSampler(MetricsSource metricsSource, ClusterSeriesStore seriesStore)
    Reset-aware sampler that feeds the cluster series ring from the merged aggregate; shared-store mode only.
    ClusterSeriesStore
    clusterSeriesStore()
    Bounded, retention-pruned ring holding the cluster-wide trend (design §5.4); shared-store mode only.
    ClusterSnapshotController

    Ingest controller for peer snapshot pushes; present only in shared-store mode.

    DashboardMetricsController

    DashboardMetricsService
    dashboardMetricsService(FailoverMetricsSnapshotService snapshotService)
    Metrics service + controller — present only when a MeterRegistry is in the context.
    FailoverMetricsSnapshotService
    failoverMetricsSnapshotService(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry registry)
    Standalone fallback FailoverMetricsSnapshotService for dashboard-only deployments where failover-spring-boot-autoconfigure is not on the classpath.
    FailoverScanner
    failoverScanner()
    Standalone fallback: when the failover library is absent (the dashboard runs as its own app pointed at a remote backend), supply an EmptyFailoverScanner so the config view is empty rather than failing to start.
    InstanceIdResolver
    instanceIdResolver(org.springframework.core.env.Environment environment)
    Fallback InstanceIdResolver for standalone-dashboard deployments where failover-spring-boot-autoconfigure is not on the classpath.
    MetricsSource
    metricsSource(DashboardMetricsService metricsService, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<DashboardHistoryService> history, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<SnapshotStore> snapshotStore, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<ClusterSeriesStore> seriesStore, InstanceIdResolver instanceIdResolver)
    The metrics provenance seam (see the distributed-dashboard design).
    SnapshotStore
    snapshotStore()
    In-memory snapshot store for cluster.mode=shared-store (design §5) — the default store=inmemory.

  • Constructor Details

    • DashboardAutoConfiguration

      public DashboardAutoConfiguration(DashboardProperties properties)

  • Method Details

    • failoverScanner

      @Bean @ConditionalOnMissingBean(FailoverScanner.class) public FailoverScanner failoverScanner()
      Standalone fallback: when the failover library is absent (the dashboard runs as its own app pointed at a remote backend), supply an EmptyFailoverScanner so the config view is empty rather than failing to start. The real scanner wins whenever the failover library is present (this autoconfig is ordered after it).

    • dashboardConfigService

      @Bean @ConditionalOnMissingBean public DashboardConfigService dashboardConfigService(FailoverScanner scanner, org.springframework.core.env.Environment environment)

    • dashboardController

      @Bean @ConditionalOnMissingBean public DashboardController dashboardController(DashboardConfigService configService)

    • instanceIdResolver

      @Bean @ConditionalOnMissingBean public InstanceIdResolver instanceIdResolver(org.springframework.core.env.Environment environment)
      Fallback InstanceIdResolver for standalone-dashboard deployments where failover-spring-boot-autoconfigure is not on the classpath. When the full failover starter is present, FailoverMicrometerAutoConfiguration supplies a DefaultInstanceIdResolver first and this bean is skipped.

    • failoverMetricsSnapshotService

      @Bean @ConditionalOnBean(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public FailoverMetricsSnapshotService failoverMetricsSnapshotService(io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry registry)
      Standalone fallback FailoverMetricsSnapshotService for dashboard-only deployments where failover-spring-boot-autoconfigure is not on the classpath. When the full failover starter is present, FailoverMicrometerAutoConfiguration contributes the real service first and this fallback is skipped.

    • dashboardMetricsService

      @Bean @ConditionalOnBean({io.micrometer.core.instrument.MeterRegistry.class,FailoverMetricsSnapshotService.class}) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public DashboardMetricsService dashboardMetricsService(FailoverMetricsSnapshotService snapshotService)
      Metrics service + controller — present only when a MeterRegistry is in the context. Without Micrometer the config view above still works; the metrics view degrades gracefully (§3).

    • metricsSource

      @Bean @ConditionalOnBean(DashboardMetricsService.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public MetricsSource metricsSource(DashboardMetricsService metricsService, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<DashboardHistoryService> history, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<SnapshotStore> snapshotStore, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<ClusterSeriesStore> seriesStore, InstanceIdResolver instanceIdResolver)
      The metrics provenance seam (see the distributed-dashboard design). Defaults to the local registry (this instance only); cluster.mode=prometheus with a base-url aggregates the failover.* meters cluster-wide via the Prometheus HTTP API, falling back to local at runtime if Prometheus is unreachable. @ConditionalOnMissingBean so a consumer can fully override it.

    • snapshotStore

      @Bean @ConditionalOnExpression("\'${failover.dashboard.cluster.mode:local}\' == \'shared-store\' and \'${failover.dashboard.cluster.shared-store.store:inmemory}\' == \'inmemory\'") @ConditionalOnMissingBean public SnapshotStore snapshotStore()
      In-memory snapshot store for cluster.mode=shared-store (design §5) — the default store=inmemory. store=jdbc instead activates the durable failover-dashboard-snapshotstore-jdbc module. @ConditionalOnMissingBean so a consumer can supply a distributed implementation.

    • clusterSnapshotController

      @Bean @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="failover.dashboard.cluster", name="mode", havingValue="shared-store") @ConditionalOnBean(SnapshotStore.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public ClusterSnapshotController clusterSnapshotController(SnapshotStore snapshotStore)
      Ingest controller for peer snapshot pushes; present only in shared-store mode.

    • clusterSeriesStore

      @Bean @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="failover.dashboard.cluster", name="mode", havingValue="shared-store") @ConditionalOnMissingBean public ClusterSeriesStore clusterSeriesStore()
      Bounded, retention-pruned ring holding the cluster-wide trend (design §5.4); shared-store mode only.

    • clusterSeriesSampler

      @Bean(destroyMethod="close") @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="failover.dashboard.cluster", name="mode", havingValue="shared-store") @ConditionalOnBean({MetricsSource.class,ClusterSeriesStore.class}) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public ClusterSeriesSampler clusterSeriesSampler(MetricsSource metricsSource, ClusterSeriesStore seriesStore)
      Reset-aware sampler that feeds the cluster series ring from the merged aggregate; shared-store mode only. Closed on shutdown to stop its scheduler. Depends on the assembled MetricsSource (the shared source).

    • dashboardMetricsController

      @Bean @ConditionalOnBean(MetricsSource.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean public DashboardMetricsController dashboardMetricsController(MetricsSource metricsSource)

    • addInterceptors

      public void addInterceptors(org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.InterceptorRegistry registry)
      Enforces exposure.include narrowing and the CSP header on every base-path/** request.
      Specified by:
      addInterceptors in interface org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer

    • addResourceHandlers

      public void addResourceHandlers(@NonNull org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ResourceHandlerRegistry registry)
      Specified by:
      addResourceHandlers in interface org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer

    • addViewControllers

      public void addViewControllers(@NonNull org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.ViewControllerRegistry registry)
      Welcome mapping: a bare base-path (and its trailing-slash form) forwards to the static index.html, so /failover-dashboard opens the UI without the explicit file name. A forward (not redirect) keeps the URL clean and lets the resource handler serve the page. Skipped when UI exposure is narrowed off.
      Specified by:
      addViewControllers in interface org.springframework.web.servlet.config.annotation.WebMvcConfigurer
